zh.lifeSummary: 當John Ammon Carter於大約1550年出生在英國米德萊可倫敦時，他的父親John Carter22歲，母親Agnes Edlyn20歲。他於1571年5月16日與Joane Woodman在布利提申 韋斯特 英迪斯特立尼達及多巴哥維多利亞聖 瑪格麗特結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於大約1580年在英國英格蘭倫敦維斯特明斯特享年32歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当John Ammon Carter于大约1550年出生在英国米德萊可倫敦时，他的父亲John Carter22岁，母亲Agnes Edlyn20岁。他于1571年5月16日与Joane Woodman在布利提申 韋斯特 英迪斯特立尼达和多巴哥維多利亞聖 瑪格麗特结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于大约1580年在英国英格兰倫敦維斯特明斯特享年32岁。

nameMeanings.name: Carter
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for a transporter of goods, from Middle English carter(e) ‘carter’ (Anglo-Norman French car(e)tier, Old French charetier, medieval Latin carettarius, carettator). The Old French word coalesced with the earlier Middle English word cart(e) ‘cart’, which is from either Old Norse kartr or Old English cræt, both of which, like the Late Latin word, were probably derived from Celtic. This surname is also very common among African Americans.

variants.name: Charman
meanings: English (Sussex and Surrey): occupational name for a carter, carrier, or coachman, from Middle English (Old French) char, car ‘car, cart, carriage’ + Middle English man ‘man’. Compare Carter and Carman 2.

English (Sussex and Surrey): occupational name for someone who did odd jobs, from Middle English char(re), chere (Old English cier(r), cer(r), cear(r), cær(r)) ‘turn (of activity), small job, chore’ + man. Compare Carman 2.
In some cases probably also an Americanized form of Slovenian Čarman (see Carman 5).

variants.name: Chartier
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Armand, Normand, Jacques, Marcel, Adelard, Alcide, Emile, Gilles, Raoul, Rosaire, Alphonse.
French: shortened form of charretier, an occupational name for a carter, from an agent derivative of Old French charette ‘cart’.
French Canadian: altered form of English   Carter  , a cognate of 1 above. Compare   Sharkey   and   Shorkey  .

variants.name: Karter
meanings: German: occupational name for a carder (someone who prepares wool for spinning), from Middle High German karter. Compare Carter .

variants.name: McCarter
meanings: Scottish and northern Irish: variant of   McArthur  .

variants.name: Carrier
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Andre, Marcel, Emile, Lucien, Cecile, Alain, Gaetan, Gilles, Jacques, Laurent, Normand.

English and southern French: from Middle English, Old French car(r)ier (from Late Latin carrarius, a derivative of carrum ‘cart, wagon’, a word of Gaulish origin), an occupational name for a carter (see Carter ), in French also for a cartwright. Compare French Charrier .
French: occupational name for a stonemason or quarryman, carrier. Compare Coyer .

variants.name: Cartman
meanings: English (Yorkshire and Lancashire): occupational name from Middle English cartman ‘man who operated a cart for the transport of goods’. Compare Carter .

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

nameMeanings.name: Ammon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Mainly Jewish: biblical name (meaning ‘faithful’ in Hebrew), borne by King David's eldest son, who raped and abandoned his half-sister Tamar and was killed by her brother Absolom.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

en.lifeSummary: When John Ammon Carter was born about 1550, in London, Middlesex, England, his father, John Carter, was 22 and his mother, Agnes Edlyn, was 20. He married Joane Woodman on 16 May 1571, in Saint Margaret, Victoria, Trinidad and Tobago, British West Indies. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. He died about 1580, in Westminster, London, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 32.

Il a épousé Joane Woodman le 16 mai 1571, à Saint Margaret, Victoria, Trinité-et-Tobago, British West Indies. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé environ 1580, à Westminster, Londres, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 32 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: John Ammon Carter(이)가 대략 1550년 영국 미들레세스 런던에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Carter(은)는 22세였고, 그의 어머니 Agnes Edlyn(은)는 20세였다. 그는 1571년 5월 16일 브리티시 웨스트 인디스 트리니다드 토바고 빅토리아 세인트 마거릿에서 Joane Woodman(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 대략 1580년 영국 잉글랜드 런던 웨스트민스터에서 32세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando John Ammon Carter nasceu aproximadamente 1550, em London, Middlesex, Inglaterra, seu pai, John Carter, tinha 22 anos e sua mãe, Agnes Edlyn, tinha 20 anos. Ele casou-se com Joane Woodman em 16 de maio de 1571, em Saint Margaret, Victoria, Trinidade e Tobago, British West Indies. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha. Ele faleceu aproximadamente 1580, em Westminster, London, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 32 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда John Ammon Carter родился около 1550 г. в местности Лондон, миддлесекс, Англия, его отцу по имени John Carter было 22 года, а матери, которую звали Agnes Edlyn, 20. Он женился на женщине по имени Joane Woodman 16 мая 1571 г. в местности сеинт маргарет, викторя, Тринидад и Тобаго, бритиш вест индиесь. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Он умер около 1580 г. в местности вестминстир, Лондон, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 32 лет.
